info.title: Bytehide
info.description: Protect your .NET applications from the terminal, use Shield and other products with a few commands.

commands.name: shield
commands.description: All the functions that Bytehide shield offers

options.name: --help
options.description: Prints help information

commands.name: check
commands.description:
Check if the
application is
protected

options.name: --help
options.description: Prints help information

commands.name: protect
commands.description:
Protect your
application with a
single execution,
ideal for configuring
your scripts and
automating msbuild

options.name: --help
options.description: Prints help information

options.name: --pdb-path
options.description:
Path of the debug symbols (.pdb) path. If not
provided, Shield will not generate a new .pdb
file

options.name: --preset
options.description:
Selects the type of preset maximum, balance or
optimized that should be applied

options.name: --configuration
options.description:
Path of the application shield configuration. If
not provided, the default configuration will be
used

options.name: --sign-key
options.description:
Path of the sign key to sign the application. If
not provided, the application will not be signed
If not provided, the application will not be
signed

options.name: --sign-key-password
options.description:
Password of the sign key to sign the application.
If not provided, the application will not be
signed

options.name: --project-token
options.description:
The project token to link this protection.
Ignored if configuration file provided, required
if configuration is not present

options.name: --protections
options.description:
The protection IDs split by ',' (comma). I.e:
rename,anti_debug

commands.name: protect-asp-javascript
commands.description:
Apply obfuscation to
JavaScript files in
your ASP.NET
application. This
command allows you to
secure both
user-defined and core
ASP.NET scripts in a
single execution,
making it ideal for
automated protection
in your build process

options.name: --help
options.description: Prints help information

options.name: --replace-project
options.description:
If set to true, the original project files
will be replaced with the obfuscated
versions
scripts (e.g., webforms.js) in addition to
user-defined scripts
name where the obfuscated project will be
saved

options.name: --obfuscate-aspcore
options.description:
Indicates whether to obfuscate core ASP.NET
scripts (e.g., webforms.js) in addition to
user-defined scripts

options.name: --output-directory-prefix
options.description:
Specifies a prefix for the output directory
name where the obfuscated project will be
saved

options.name: --output-directory
options.description:
Specifies the path of the output directory
where the obfuscated project will be saved.
If not provided, a default directory will
be used

commands.name: protect-javascript
commands.description:
Apply obfuscation to
JavaScript files. This
command allows you to
secure .js scripts in
a single execution,
making it ideal for
automated protection
in your build process

options.name: --help
options.description:
Prints help information
file names of the directory that must be
obfuscated

options.name: --script-match
options.description:
Specifies the regex expression to match the
file names of the directory that must be
obfuscated

options.name: --replace-project
options.description:
If set to true, the original project files
will be replaced with the obfuscated
versions
name where the obfuscated project will be
saved

options.name: --output-directory-prefix
options.description:
Specifies a prefix for the output directory
name where the obfuscated project will be
saved

options.name: --output-directory
options.description:
Specifies the path of the output directory
where the obfuscated project will be saved.
If not provided, a default directory will
be used

options.name: --help
options.description: Prints help information

options.name: --version
options.description: Prints version information
